title_guess: Abbey Dore / Golden Valley & Hanley Swan (Malvern)

pages.text:
Relations were coming to visit [illegible] at home, so we wanted to return quickly. But it rained heavily all the way. We travelled at 30 mph with huge traffic jams & only reached home after 2 hours.

    April 24th Friday.
S,E,J,C,5·30. we left to camp west of Hereford. We went down the M5, west on the M50, around the south of the Malverns. We are staying at __Abbey Dore__, at the end of __Golden Valley__. The campsite is below the Neville Arms pub. We arrived at 7.30. Simon had booked supper for 8.15., but after making the beds, we ate straight away. We had pheasant & beef & treacle tart & custard & beers.

    April 25th
    It rained in the night, but glorious sunshine & blue sky at breakfast, so we were almost too hot.
    We walked to see the Abbey 1147, & then on across the __Golden Valley__ to the __Abbey Dore Court Gardens__, which were very pretty. They had a Teddy collection too.

The Abbey Dore building was started in the reign of Stephen 1135-54, on land which was then in the parish of Bocton, given to the Cistercians by a grandson of William I. The order had been founded in Citeaux in Burgundy in 1128. Its aims were simplicity & austerity: Farming rather than learning.
	Dore came from the Welsh "dour," water. Abbey by the water.
	At the Abbey was a woman serving coffee, who said that she had grandchildren at Blue Coat. She is

__Screen__

THIS IS A VERY FINE piece of Renaissance woodwork, almost certainly the work of John Abel, 1577-64. Surviving works which are

Jo McDonald's mother!
	Apparently Charles I's arms are in the middle of the screen, & the arms of the family who stole the monastery - Scudamore & another, are [in] place of the thieves!
	Beautiful stonework, but much of it has disappeared. They were having a [G]regorian Chant workshop day.
	We left after lunch: or rather we

places: St. Gabriel's church of the good shepherd
pages.text:
tried to leave after lunch. But we got bogged down - The publican arrived with a small Peugeot & also got bogged down, while making us more bogged. Eventually 6 men, who were on a llama-training course up the road, pulled us both out. Reports say it has been the wettest April this century.
     We reached __Blackmore__ *** campsite at __Hanley Swan__, near Malvern. Peter & Thomas played well in the concert for the official opening of St. Edmunds Concert Hall, Thomas did a solo & sang. We drank & chatted afterwards.
     April 26th Sunday
     We woke & drove to St. Gabriel's church of the good shepherd to have breakfast. 2 women led the service. It was the Brownie parade. It was an excellent family service - Charles claimed that it was the first sermon that he had understood, about the bad habits of sheep & characteristics of the shepherd.
     We drove down to the humsdens,
